activity.description: one-line summaries for the README's recent-activity digest lines
activity.key_hint: <EventType>:<owner/repo> for dossier events worth summarizing (Push, PullRequest, Release, Create, Issues) — e.g. PushEvent:octocat/Hello-World
activity.as_of_hint: pushes: the event's 12-char head sha; other events: the event date
activity.raw_material: Run `python3 .github/scripts/update_profile.py harvest --out <tmp>/dossier.json` and read recent_events (deduped digest, newest first — push entries carry a 12-char head sha; PR/issue/release entries may carry a title). The events API no longer ships commit messages, so per PushEvent repo fetch `gh api "repos/<owner/repo>/commits?since=<30 days ago>&per_page=20"` and collect the commit subject lines.

shipped.description: one-line summaries for the README's recently-shipped release lines, preferred over the bare release name
shipped.key_hint: <repo>@<tag> for releases inside the shipped window — e.g. Hello-World@v2.1.0
shipped.raw_material: The dossier's releases each carry name and a truncated body. A body with real prose can be used directly. When the body is an autogenerated changelog link (`**Full Changelog**: .../compare/A...B`), fetch the real content via `gh api "repos/<owner/repo>/compare/A...B"` (commit subjects), or fall back to the release's commits.
